title: Anthropic Blocks Foreigners From Using Mythos and Fable AI
description: The company said on Friday night that the federal government had ordered limits on its Mythos and Fable 5 A.I. systems, citing national security concerns.
author: Cade Metz and Dustin Volz

title: What the SpaceX I.P.O. Means for OpenAI and Anthropic
description: Strong investor interest in SpaceX could be good news for the A.I. rivals, which have signaled that they, too, intend to go public this year.
author: Mike Isaac

title: SpaceX Stock Rises 11% in Largest IPO Ever
description: The blockbuster stock market debut of Elon Musk’s rocket company paves the way for a wave of enormous offerings that are coming from OpenAI and Anthropic.
author: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch

title: SpaceX’s Unlikely Journey From Far-Out Idea to $2 Trillion Juggernaut
description: Elon Musk said he had initially given SpaceX less than a 10 percent chance of succeeding. His rocket company has come a long way.
author: Ryan Mac, Kenneth Chang and Kirsten Grind

title: Elon Musk Becomes World’s First Trillionaire as SpaceX Stock Begins Trading
description: With SpaceX shares soaring 20 percent on their first day of trading, the world’s richest person crossed another milestone — one with 13 digits.
author: Ryan Mac and Ben Casselman

title: If You Have a 401(k), How Much SpaceX Stock Will You Own?
description: If you have index funds — such as in a 401(k) — they may soon include shares of the mega company. Here’s a rough calculation.
author: C.J. Robinson

title: ‘Hard Fork’ Live, Part 1: Satya Nadella and Cindy Cohn
description: A tech titan, a digital rights defender and two menacing-looking robot dogs took the stage at our second-ever live show.
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Vjeran Pavic, Alyssa Moxley, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano, Rowan Niemisto and Diane Wong

title: Google Sues to Stop Chinese Cybercrime Group from Using Its A.I.
description: In a lawsuit, the tech giant accused the group of using Google’s Gemini system to create hundreds of fake corporate and government websites.
author: Cecilia Kang

title: SpaceX Finalizes IPO Price at $135 a Share in World’s Largest Public Offering
description: Elon Musk’s rocket company said it would sell more than 555 million shares at $135 each in its blockbuster initial public offering, which is set to begin trading on Friday.
author: Ryan Mac

title: SpaceX IPO: How Our Reporters Assess the Sky-High Valuation and Potential Economic Impact
description: Two Times tech reporters and their editor talk about assessing the sky-high valuation of the rocket and satellite maker, the potential economic impact of its public debut and the role of the world’s richest man in it all.
author: Megan DiTrolio

title: Skeptics Question Whether SpaceX Is Worth $1.77 Trillion
description: Elon Musk’s rocket company is spending big and losing money. That has raised questions about whether it can justify its valuation for its blockbuster initial public offering.
author: Ryan Mac and Mike Isaac

title: Jeff Bezos Wants to Build an ‘Artificial General Engineer’
description: As co-chief executive of the start-up Prometheus, the Amazon founder is using A.I. to improve how devices ranging from computers to jet engines are made.
author: Cade Metz

title: How Tesla’s Stock Listing in 2010 Enabled SpaceX’s I.P.O.
description: Investors who bought the automaker’s shares in its initial public offering became rich. As a result, many have a deep faith in Elon Musk, the chief executive of Tesla and SpaceX.
author: Jack Ewing

title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I.
description: At The New York Times’s Hard Fork Live event, Mr. Nadella addressed the backlash against artificial intelligence and President Trump’s comments about Americans sharing in the wealth of A.I. companies
author: Emmy Martin

title: Britain Is Weighing a Social Media Ban for Children. How Did It Get Here?
description: Months after Australia banned social media for everyone under 16, the British government is considering new policies to keep children safe online.
author: Megan Specia

title: Trump Muses About Government Taking a Piece of A.I. Companies
description: President Trump touched on what is an increasingly hot topic in Washington: how average Americans can get a piece of the tech industry’s A.I. windfall.
author: Tripp Mickle, Ana Swanson and Cecilia Kang

title: SpaceX’s IPO Could Turn 4,400 Employees Into Millionaires
description: While Elon Musk may soon become a trillionaire, his rocket company’s market debut is set to change the lives of its current and former employees, too.
author: Kirsten Grind

title: They Tried To Catch a Child Predator on a Livestream. They Trapped Themselves Instead.
description: What happened when livestreaming vigilantes ambushed an innocent man.
author: Kashmir Hill and Aric Toler

title: People Love to Hate This Fake Private Equity Guy
description: Johnny Hilbrant Partridge has found social media fame as PE Guy, a character who embodies obnoxious entitlement.
author: Guy Trebay

title: Meta A.I. Bug Allowed Hackers to Take Over Instagram Accounts
description: The flaw, which Meta said it had fixed, allowed anyone to take over accounts using a bug in the company’s new artificial intelligence software.
author: Mike Isaac and Eli Tan
